Crude Oil ETF Flow Signal
← Back to dashboard

Crude Oil ETF Flow Signal

Approximate IEO capital-flow monitor built from iShares Historical shares outstanding data. This is an oil-exposure proxy through exploration-and-production equities, not a physical crude holdings flow.

As of 2026-04-14

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement (mandatory): Given the contraction in Shares Outstanding to 5.05M, a sharply negative z_price_20d of -3.07, and a Neutral flow_regime, this setup aligns with Range-biased price paths and Elevated volatility, where the dominant risk is Mean reversion, not Trend continuation. (1)
  • The signal has transitioned from a period of strong accumulation into a Neutral state characterized by significant price-flow divergence. (1)
  • Conviction Band: Low; Interpretation Confidence: Mixed Signals; Internal Conflict Flag: Yes. Signal Stability Assessment: Deteriorating; Threshold Proximity: Near; Revision Sensitivity: Low. (1)

Methodology Applied:

  • Capital flow pressure is interpreted via changes in Shares Outstanding (flow_shares) and NAV-weighted USD flows. (1)
  • Regime classification (e.g., Accumulation) is determined by the Divergence metric between price action and capital flows. (1)
  • Positive divergence (Accumulation regime) implies price is falling faster than capital is exiting. (1)
  • Crude Oil ETF Flow Signal (2026-04-14). (1)

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the extreme negative price momentum (z_price_20d: -3.07) relative to moderate share destruction (-100k shares). (1)
  • Internal conflict exists between the medium-term strength (z_flow_60d: 1.11) and the immediate tactical deterioration. (1)
  • Conditional Invalidation: Transition of the divergence_regime from Accumulation to Distribution. (1)
  • Stability is deteriorating as the signal flipped from Strong Inflow to Neutral within the current month. (1)

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant; risks balanced due to extreme price dislocation against resilient medium-term flows. (1)
  • Upside risk: A "mean reversion" rally triggered by the exhaustion of tactical selling while institutional support persists. (1)
  • Downside risk: A "regime shift" where Neutral flows accelerate into "Strong Outflow," validating the price crash. (1)

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks), reflecting the rapid shift in z-scores and share outstanding changes. (1)
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium, as the signal captures upstream capital-flow sentiment before it hits broader energy indices. (1)

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the energy-sector liquidity dimension and risk appetite for commodity-linked equities. (1)
  • Implied mechanism: Capital-flow pressure suggests a potential floor if accumulation divergence holds. (1)
  • Cycle position: Not determined. (1)

Regime Context:

  • The current Neutral regime is newly entered, following a sustained period of Strong Inflow. (1)
  • Direction of change: Weakening. (1)

Model Limitations:

  • Flows track energy-equity proxy (IEO) rather than physical crude oil holdings. (1)
  • Revision window cannot be assessed from provided content.

Data & References:

  • Latest observation: 2026-04-14. (1)
  • Most influential: flow_shares (-100k) and z_price_20d (-3.07). (1)
  • Public datasets: CFTC positioning data and physical crude inventory levels would improve cross-validation.

Crude Oil ETF Flow (IEO Proxy)

Approximate flow signal derived from changes in IEO shares outstanding and NAV per share. This is a capital-flow proxy for crude exposure.
